title: RTOS Basics VIII (A Practical Guide): Priority Inversion
description: In today’s final installment of the RTOS Basics series, we explore the root causes of priority inversion errors, learn how to measure them, and discover how to prevent them using FreeRTOS, STM32, and SystemView.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: RTOS Basics VIII (A Practical Guide): Priority Inversion
description: In today’s final installment of the RTOS Basics series, we explore the root causes of priority inversion errors, learn how to measure them, and discover how to prevent them using FreeRTOS, STM32, and SystemView.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: RTOS Basics VII (A Practical Guide): Deadlock
description: When two tasks end up waiting forever... something went wrong in the design. In this edition, I explain what a deadlock is, how it happens in an RTOS, and how to prevent it. We review it with a practical example using FreeRTOS, STM32 and SystemView.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: RTOS Basics VI (A Practical Guide): Event Flags
description: Event flags provide a lightweight and efficient way to signal events between tasks. In this edition, we’ll explore them through a practical example using STM32 Nucleo and FreeRTOS.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: RTOS Basics V (A Practical Guide): Queues
description: Queues are the go-to method for passing data between tasks in a safe and orderly way. In this issue, we’ll explore how they work in practice using an STM32 Nucleo board and FreeRTOS.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: RTOS Basics IV (A Practical Guide): Semaphores
description: In an RTOS, semaphores are a key mechanism for synchronizing tasks and waiting for events. In this edition, I’ll explain the two main types in a visual way, using an STM32 Nucleo board, FreeRTOS, and SystemView.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: RTOS Basics III (A Practical Guide): Mutexes
description: In RTOS, mutexes are the most common way to protect shared resources, prevent race conditions, and coordinate multiple tasks. I’ll explain them in a simple and visual way, using an STM32 Nucleo board, FreeRTOS, and SystemView.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: RTOS Basics II (A Practical Guide): Tasks and Scheduler
description: Let’s dive into the practical part! To truly understand what the scheduler does, we’re going to configure it in two different modes to compare them: preemptive and cooperative. I’ll walk you through a real example using an STM32 Nucleo board, FreeRTOS, and SystemView.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: RTOS Basics I (Guía práctica): Introducción
description: Nueva serie! Como nunca está de más repasar las bases antes de empezar con algo nuevo (Zephyr), vamos a hablar de RTOS y por qué son clave en software embebido. Hoy empezamos con una introducción, pero en los próximos posts nos pondremos a cacharrear. Nada de teoría aburrida! Usaré una tarjeta STM32 Nucleo con un MCU STM32F401RE, FreeRTOS y SystemView.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter

title: From Code to Executable V
description: In this final chapter of the "From Code to Executable" series, we take a practical look at the build process of an STM32.
author: Embedded Systems Hub | Newsletter
